# AGENTS.md — Designflow Backend: Primary Developer Guide

Read this file in full before touching any code. It is the authoritative orientation document for any developer or AI session working on this repository.

---

## 1. What this service is

Designflow Backend is the main REST API for the Designflow PLM platform. It is a stateless Node.js + Express service backed by PostgreSQL (Sequelize ORM). Identity comes from a JWT on every request; session state does not exist. The service runs on Google Cloud Run and is reached exclusively through the BFF (`designflow-bff`), which terminates the user JWT and adds an OIDC service-to-service token before forwarding. The frontend is a separate Angular SPA in a sibling repository. For a full system diagram see `docs/architecture.md`.

---

## 2. Multi-model AI note

There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.

`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.

When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.

---

## 2a. Documentation map: what to read for each task

Always start with this file (`AGENTS.md`). Then load other docs **only when relevant** — do
not ingest every `.md`.

| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
|---|---|---|
| Quick orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | deep `docs/` |
| Modify endpoints / model / domain logic | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md` | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy changes |
| Add/change config, env vars, secrets, app settings | `AGENTS.md` (§12), `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod runtime affected | unrelated architecture docs |
| DB schema / migration / new column / data flow | `AGENTS.md` (§6 Decision tree, §8 Data model), `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/configuration.md` if env affected | `docs/deployment.md` unless rollout changes |
| Local setup, run, test, debug | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md` | `docs/deployment.md` |
| Deploy / Docker / Cloud Build / Cloud Run / rollback | `AGENTS.md` (§13), `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md` | `docs/development.md` |
| AI proxy (OpenRouter / DeepSeek), tariffs (Section 301 / Trump 2025), HTS | `AGENTS.md` (§3, §11), `docs/architecture.md` | local-only dev docs |
| MCP server work | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/mcp-integration.md` | unrelated module docs |
| Internal REST endpoint reference | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/api-reference.md`, `routes/lib.router.js` | deployment docs |
| Investigate a bug/incident | `AGENTS.md` (§14 Critical incident log), the area's doc, `HANDOFF.md` if present | unrelated docs |
| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, **`HANDOFF.md`** (present — required reading), docs it names | docs outside the handoff scope |
| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | other docs unless needed |
| Docs-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected `docs/` | source except to verify |

`HANDOFF.md` currently exists (factory bulk offer upload, SYS-104/105) and is required reading
for continuation work. Update this table whenever a doc is added, removed, or repurposed.

---

## 3. Repository structure

**What happened:** RFQ grid column headers (and much more) rendered as garbage — `备注` showed as boxes/junk, `中国` as `Σ╕¡σ¢╜`, `Décor` as `D├⌐cor`, `Cortázar` as `Cort├ízar`, `©` as `┬⌐`, `'` as `ΓÇÖ`. The 12 visible RFQ header rows were the tip; a tripwire scan (looking for the box-drawing/garbage bytes that never occur in real data) flagged corruption in **thousands of additional rows across ~20+ text columns** — vendor/factory country, item descriptions, user last names, merch group, licensing feedback, vendor notes/addresses, audit-log history, email templates.

**Root cause:** The entire `designflow_dev` schema was hand-loaded through a Windows console (cmd.exe) step whose code page is **CP437**, not UTF-8. Every non-ASCII byte sequence was reinterpreted through CP437 on the way in and stored that way — classic mojibake. This is a *data-at-rest* corruption from the load, **not** an application bug.

**Scope / isolation:** Confined to the `designflow_dev` sandbox schema. The clean schemas (`designflow`, `designflow_sandbox`) had **zero** hits — the master data was never routed through the bad console step. The conservative tripwire only catches values containing box-drawing junk; a few mojibake classes (e.g. mangled smart-quotes `ΓÇÖ`) lack those bytes, so any count is a **floor, not a ceiling**.

**Recovery:** The 12 RFQ header rows were repaired in place (live). A broader in-place repair of the remaining rows was scoped — re-encode each genuinely-garbled value (CP437→UTF-8 round-trip), skip already-clean values, and **leave image-link columns alone** (`RFQItem.rfqItem_picture` / `rfqItem_picturethumb`, `itemAttachment.attachment_link` / `item_attachment_fileName`): "correcting" a URL that points at a file actually stored under the garbled name would break the image. **Status of the bulk row repair is unconfirmed — verify with a fresh tripwire scan before assuming it ran** (a prior session that started it did not persist any of its work).

**Prevention (in place):** `client_encoding: 'UTF8'` added to `dialectOptions` in both `designflow-backend/models/db.js` and `designflow-data-syncing/models/db.js`. This forces UTF-8 on the connection so the app can't introduce new mojibake; it does **not** retro-fix existing data.

**Rule — re-seeding `designflow_dev`:** Never pipe a SQL/CSV dump into the database through `cmd.exe` (or any CP437 console). Use a UTF-8-safe path: `pg_dump`/`pg_restore` in custom/directory format, or `psql` with `\copy` and `client_encoding=UTF8` (e.g. `psql "...options=-c client_encoding=UTF8"` or `SET client_encoding TO 'UTF8';` before `\copy`). If you must use a console, run `chcp 65001` (UTF-8 code page) first and confirm the file itself is UTF-8. After any re-seed, run the tripwire scan to confirm zero garbage bytes. See also `designflow-data-syncing/README.md` → "Data encoding (UTF-8)".

---

## OpenRouter proxy

`helpers/openrouter.js`:
- `listUserModels()` — 12 s timeout
- `chatCompletion({ model, messages, max_tokens, temperature, session_id })` — 90 s timeout

`helpers/deepseek.js` and `helpers/qwen.js` provide direct provider paths for `api='deepseek-direct'` and `api='qwen-direct'`.

### AI proxy route auth

What changed:
`routes/openrouter.router.js` now gates both `/models` and `/chat` with `authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production'])`.

Why:
These routes spend paid OpenRouter/DeepSeek/Qwen quota, so they must not be callable anonymously.

Future sessions should:
Keep route-level auth on both AI proxy endpoints and verify it with `tests/unit/openrouter.router.test.js` when changing provider routing.

### AI cache strategy metadata

What changed:
`helpers/cache-strategy.js` annotates AI models as `automatic-prefix`, `explicit-cache-control`, `session-cache`, or `none/unknown`. The frontend AI Settings page reads these fields to show cache eligibility and provider-specific validation.

Why:
OpenAI, DeepSeek, OpenRouter-hosted providers, and Alibaba Qwen do not share one caching mechanism. Treating them as equivalent can either send useless cache markers or miss the cheaper provider-native cache path.

Future sessions should:
Keep cache behavior driven by model metadata instead of hard-coding UI assumptions. Verify provider changes with `tests/unit/openrouter.helper.test.js`, `tests/unit/qwen.helper.test.js`, and the AI Settings sandbox build.

### Qwen direct session cache

What changed:
Direct Qwen model discovery calls Alibaba Cloud Model Studio's OpenAI-compatible `/models` endpoint, not OpenRouter. Direct Qwen RFQ AI sessions use Alibaba's Responses API with `x-dashscope-session-cache: enable` and `previous_response_id` when `session_id` is supplied. One-off Qwen calls without `session_id` still fall back to Chat Completions with explicit `cache_control: { type: 'ephemeral' }` markers.

Why:
Qwen Direct must reflect models available to the configured Alibaba/DashScope account and region. Alibaba session cache is only available on the Responses API, not Chat Completions. The RFQ AI dialog already passes a stable `session_id`, so it can use session cache while one-off utility calls remain compatible with the Chat Completions endpoint.

Future sessions should:
Do not point Qwen Direct model loading at OpenRouter. Do not "simplify" Qwen direct back to Chat Completions for session calls unless Alibaba removes Responses session cache or the app stops sending `session_id`. Configure model listing and Chat Completions with `QWEN_BASE_URL` / `DASHSCOPE_BASE_URL`; configure the Responses base with `QWEN_RESPONSES_BASE_URL` / `DASHSCOPE_RESPONSES_BASE_URL`.

### Cache telemetry normalization

What changed:
`helpers/cache-metrics.js` normalizes provider usage into `prompt_cache_hit_tokens`, `prompt_cache_miss_tokens`, `prompt_cache_creation_tokens`, and hit-rate fields. The RFQ AI dialog aggregates these fields into session cache stats, estimated cost, and estimated cache savings.

Why:
Provider usage shapes differ (`cached_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, DeepSeek hit/miss fields, etc.), but the UI needs one stable telemetry contract.

Future sessions should:
When adding a provider, first map its usage fields in `helpers/cache-metrics.js`; then expose `cache_strategy` metadata so the AI Settings validation remains accurate. If a provider returns no cache-specific telemetry, leave `prompt_cache_telemetry_available=false` rather than inventing hits.

---

## Notification system

Stored in `user_notification` table (`id`, `type`, `event`, `title`, `message`, `unread`, `created_date`, `user_id_fk`).

**Service methods** (`services/notification.service.js`):
- `getNotifications(userId, limit=20)` — unread rows ordered by `created_date DESC`
- `getUnreadCount(userId)` — count of unread rows
- `createNotification({ userId, type, event, title, message })` — single insert
- `createManyNotifications(userIds, payload)` — bulk insert (used by @mention pipeline)
- `markAsRead(id, userId)`, `markAllAsRead(userId)` — set `unread = false`
- `getMentionableUsers()` — active users for @mention autocomplete

**@mention pipeline (triggered by `addComment`):**
1. `parseMentions(text)` extracts `@FirstName` / `@FirstName.LastName` tokens
2. `resolveMentions(handles, users)` matches to active user IDs
3. `createManyNotifications(targets, payload)` bulk-inserts; author excluded
4. Fires async after the comment response is sent — errors do not block the caller

**Frontend integration:**
- `NotificationService` polls `GET /api/notifications/count` every 30 s; exposes `unreadCount$` observable
- Toolbar badge displays the live unread count
- Toolbar dropdown fetches `GET /api/notifications` on each open, maps `type` to icon, and supports mark-as-read per item and mark-all-as-read

**Known gap:** `createNotification` / `createManyNotifications` are only called from the @mention pipeline. No other business events (task assignments, status changes, approvals) currently fire notifications. The infrastructure is in place; trigger call-sites are the missing piece.

---

## Impersonation

`POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId` (admin only):
1. Verifies caller JWT has no `impersonated_by` claim (prevents double-impersonation)
2. Looks up target user by `users.id`; if body has `{ "targetType": "factory" }`, looks up the visible factory row by `Factory.id`; if body has `{ "targetType": "vendor" }`, looks up the factory rep by `vendor.vendor_id`
3. For visible factory-row targets: signs a vendor-scoped token with `role: "vendor"`, `access`, `factory_id: Factory.id`, and `email: null` when no concrete vendor email is available. This is the correct Factories & Reps flow; do not require the frontend to expand a nested rep/detail row.
4. For vendor-row targets: signs a vendor-scoped token with `access` and `factory_id: vendor.factory_id_fk` so vendor routes filter to that factory.
5. Signs RS256 JWT (2 h TTL) with target user's claims + `impersonated_by: <adminUserId>` for audit
6. Returns `{ token }`

Frontend swaps `token` ↔ `admin_token` in localStorage. All routes automatically run as the target user — no route-level changes needed. The `impersonated_by` claim is for audit; the backend does not log impersonation events to a separate table.

**Factory-row vendor token guard:** `models/lib.model.js` must support vendor tokens whose identity is scoped by `factory_id` rather than `vendor_email`. `Customer.getRFQStep` falls back to the JWT `role` when `findUserInfoWithEmail()` cannot resolve a user/vendor row; without this fallback, visible factory-row impersonation can crash with `Cannot read properties of null (reading 'level')` and surface as `getRFQStep` / `SYS-045` failures. Keep `tests/unit/rfqStep.vendor-token.test.js` with this behavior.

---

## App settings table

`app_settings` is a key/value table managed by `AppSettingService.get(key)` / `.set(key, value)`.

| Key | Value | Written by |
|-----|-------|------------|
| `ai-model` | OpenRouter model ID string | Admin via Editor → AI Settings |
| `section301_lookup` | JSON string (~1 MB): HTS code → `{ list, rate, description }` | Admin via Section 301 CSV upload; legacy PDF endpoint still wired but UI-hidden |
| `section301_overrides` | JSON: `{ "NNNN.NN.NN": { list, rate, description? } }` — per-code corrections consulted before parsed Section 301 data | Admin via Editor → Section 301 Overrides |
| `trump_2025_tariffs` | JSON string: `[{ country, rate }, ...]` Trump 2025 reciprocal rates | Admin via Editor → Trump 2025 Tariffs |

---

## File storage (DigitalOcean Spaces)

Upload flow for customer logos and item images:

1. Frontend calls `POST /getPreSignedUrl` with `{ fileName, contentType }` → backend generates a presigned `PutObjectCommand` URL (15 min expiry, no ACL in signature)
2. Frontend PUTs the file directly to DigitalOcean Spaces using the presigned URL (browser → DO, no backend transit)
3. Frontend calls `POST /setObjectPublic` with `{ fileName }` → backend calls `s3Client.putObjectAcl({ ACL: 'public-read' })` via a direct authenticated API call → returns `{ publicUrl }`
4. Frontend saves `publicUrl` to the database record

# Deployment

## Pipeline overview

```
git push → Cloud Build trigger
  → Step 1: docker build (node:lts-alpine → npm install → CMD yarn start:${NODE_ENV})
  → Step 2: docker push → Artifact Registry
  → Step 3: gcloud run deploy → Cloud Run
  → container start: models/db.js runs inline DB migrations
```

No GitHub Actions. CI/CD is handled entirely by Google Cloud Build (`cloudbuild.yaml`).

## Dockerfile

- Base: `node:lts-alpine`
- Installs ImageMagick (for image processing endpoints)
- Copies source, runs `yarn install` (includes devDependencies for `cross-env` and `nodemon`)
- Exposes port 5000
- CMD: `yarn start:$NODE_ENV` — uses the start script matching the injected `NODE_ENV`

The backend Docker image does **not** run tests at build time (unlike the frontend), and there is no separate GitHub Actions CI. Tests gate startup only for the `start:sandbox` and `start:production` scripts (which prefix `npm test &&`); `start:sandbox-albert` runs `nodemon` with no test gate. Run `npm test` locally before pushing. See [`development.md`](development.md).

## Startup migrations

There is no separate migration job. On **every container start**, `models/db.js` (loaded via `index.js`) runs a sequence of idempotent inline migrations against the database — `ALTER TABLE "RFQItem"/"Factory"/"users"/"customers"/"vendor"/"RFQVendor" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ...`, `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS app_settings`, and a `Factory.factory_country` backfill `UPDATE`. A schema change ships by adding an `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` statement to `models/db.js`; the new column is only realized in an environment once the new revision boots.

**Mixed-case column identifiers MUST be double-quoted** in these statements. Sequelize quotes all identifiers; Postgres folds unquoted ones to lowercase. An unquoted `ADD COLUMN` created an orphan `rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots` that broke the RFQ grid (SYS-002, fixed in `a556d3f`). See AGENTS.md §14.

## Image tag format

```
{REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/{PROJECT_ID}/{ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME}/{APP_ENV}/{APP_NAME}:{BUILD_ID}-{COMMIT_SHA}
```

## Cloud Run service

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Service name | `popcre-albert-backend-sandbox` (sandbox-albert env) |
| GCP project | `lithe-breaker-323913` |
| Region | `us-central1` |
| Auth | `--no-allow-unauthenticated` (requests authenticated by BFF with OIDC token) |
| VPC connector | configured via `_VPC_CONNECTOR` trigger variable |

Secrets are injected at deploy time via `--set-secrets`; non-secret config via `--set-env-vars`. See [`configuration.md`](configuration.md) for the full variable list.

## Triggering a build

# HANDOFF — Remaining open items

Three items remain open (one is implemented but not yet committed).

---

## 1. Factory bulk offer upload — **IMPLEMENTED, NOT YET COMMITTED** (updated 2026-06-11)

A vendor-only "Upload Offers" feature was fully implemented across both `designflow-backend`
and `designflow-frontend` in this session. All code sits as unstaged changes on branch
`sandbox-albert`.

### What was built

The backend receives two new routes (vendor-only, `authRole(['vendor'])`):

| Route | Handler | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `GET /getRFQFactoryTemplate` | `SYS-104` | Fetches vendor name from JWT + all customers; returns a pre-formatted `.xlsx` buffer (ExcelJS) with hidden Lookups sheet, 50 data rows, Customer/Price Terms dropdowns, yellow image cells |
| `POST /importFactoryRFQ` | `SYS-105` | Receives `multipart/form-data` (file + groupName); parses xlsx; creates one RFQGroup + one RFQItem per row (price code `foff-XXXX`) + one RFQVendor quote per row (linked to the factory JWT's `factory_id`, `req_status=2`, step ID 3 = @factories); uploads embedded images to DigitalOcean Spaces; returns `{created, items, errors, groupId, groupName}` |

Row-level errors are bilingual (English · Chinese) and classified by Sequelize error type via `describeImportRowError()`. FK lookup failures (unknown Customer) silently set FK to null — never an error.

### Files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `helpers/rfqBulkImport.js` | **NEW** — `generateTemplate`, `parseImport`, `getNextFoffPriceCodes` |
| `helpers/digitalocean.js` | Added `uploadBufferToSpaces(buffer, fileName, contentType)` |
| `helpers/multer.js` | Added `multerMemory` (Multer memory storage, 25 MB limit) |
| `models/lib.model.js` | Added `describeImportRowError(err, row)`, `Customer.getRFQFactoryTemplate(req, cb)`, `Customer.importFactoryRFQ(req, cb)` |
| `controllers/main.controller.js` | Added `exports.getRFQFactoryTemplate` (SYS-104), `exports.importFactoryRFQ` (SYS-105) |
| `routes/lib.router.js` | Added `GET /getRFQFactoryTemplate` and `POST /importFactoryRFQ` (both `authRole(['vendor'])`) |
| `helpers/rfqCopyMerchGroupGuard.js` | Clarified the user-visible blocked-copy message to name missing/inactive Product Type / Sub-Type / Sub-Sub-Type, matching frontend tooltips/snackbars |

### Current state

- All changes are on `sandbox-albert`, **unstaged** (no `git add`, no `git commit`)
- `develop` was merged into `sandbox-albert` again on 2026-06-11; local uncommitted work was preserved
- `npm test -- --runInBand rfqCopyMerchGroupGuard` passed on 2026-06-11
- Earlier full backend suite count was 226 passing tests; rerun full `npm test` before final push if this branch stays open
- **Per push policy: do not push until Albert says so**

### Next action

When Albert says "push":
```bash
git add helpers/rfqBulkImport.js \
        helpers/digitalocean.js \
        helpers/multer.js \
        models/lib.model.js \
        controllers/main.controller.js \
        routes/lib.router.js \
        helpers/rfqCopyMerchGroupGuard.js
git commit -m "feat(rfq): factory bulk offer upload — xlsx template + import (SYS-104/105)"
git push origin sandbox-albert
```

---

Delete this file once the bulk upload is committed and pushed.

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: D:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\docs\architecture.md
@@
 ### Cache telemetry normalization
 
 What changed:
 `helpers/cache-metrics.js` normalizes provider usage into `prompt_cache_hit_tokens`, `prompt_cache_miss_tokens`, `prompt_cache_creation_tokens`, and hit-rate fields. The RFQ AI dialog aggregates these fields into session cache stats, estimated cost, and estimated cache savings.
@@
 Future sessions should:
 When adding a provider, first map its usage fields in `helpers/cache-metrics.js`; then expose `cache_strategy` metadata so the AI Settings validation remains accurate. If a provider returns no cache-specific telemetry, leave `prompt_cache_telemetry_available=false` rather than inventing hits.
+
+### Aggregate AI cache telemetry
+
+What changed:
+`POST /api/openrouter/chat` now records one row per AI provider response in `ai_cache_events` through `services/ai-cache-telemetry.service.js`. Admins read aggregate cache diagnostics with `GET /api/admin/ai-cache-stats?window=24h&feature=hts-classification`; the response includes totals plus `by_provider`, `by_model`, and `by_strategy` breakdowns.
+
+Why:
+Per-dialog cache stats were not enough to diagnose whether prompt/context caching is working across all classifications. The admin AI Settings panel needs aggregate hit rate, telemetry coverage, cached/new/write token totals, call/session counts, and provider/model breakdowns.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Keep the event table append-only and feature-tagged. HTS classification calls send `telemetry_feature='hts-classification'`; smoke tests or future helper calls should use a different feature so they do not pollute production classification metrics. If a provider returns token totals without cache counters, preserve `telemetry_available=false` instead of treating it as a cache miss.
@@
 | `ai-model` | OpenRouter model ID string | Admin via Editor → AI Settings |
+| `ai-api` | `openrouter`, `deepseek-direct`, or `qwen-direct` | Admin via Editor → AI Settings |
+| `ai-reasoning-effort` | `none`, `high`, or `max` | Admin via Editor → AI Settings |
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: D:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\docs\deployment.md
@@
-There is no separate migration job. On **every container start**, `models/db.js` (loaded via `index.js`) runs a sequence of idempotent inline migrations against the database — `ALTER TABLE "RFQItem"/"Factory"/"users"/"customers"/"vendor"/"RFQVendor" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ...`, `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS app_settings`, and a `Factory.factory_country` backfill `UPDATE`. A schema change ships by adding an `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` statement to `models/db.js`; the new column is only realized in an environment once the new revision boots.
+There is no separate migration job. On **every container start**, `models/db.js` (loaded via `index.js`) runs a sequence of idempotent inline migrations against the database — `ALTER TABLE "RFQItem"/"Factory"/"users"/"customers"/"vendor"/"RFQVendor" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ...`, `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS app_settings`, `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ai_cache_events`, and a `Factory.factory_country` backfill `UPDATE`. A schema change ships by adding an `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` or `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` statement to `models/db.js`; the new column/table is only realized in an environment once the new revision boots.
+
+### AI cache telemetry startup table
+
+What changed:
+`ai_cache_events` is created at startup and stores one row per AI provider response for aggregate cache diagnostics. It is intentionally append-only and indexed by `created_at` and `(feature, created_at)`.
+
+Why:
+Admin AI Settings needs historical cache utilization across classifications, not only per-dialog usage lines.
+
+Future sessions should:
+When modifying this table, update `models/db/AiCacheEvent.js`, the idempotent startup SQL in `models/db.js`, and `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` together. Do not rely on `sequelize.sync()` to alter existing deployed tables.
 
 **Mixed-case column identifiers MUST be double-quoted** in these statements. Sequelize quotes all identifiers; Postgres folds unquoted ones to lowercase. An unquoted `ADD COLUMN` created an orphan `rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots` that broke the RFQ grid (SYS-002, fixed in `a556d3f`). See AGENTS.md §14.
*** End Patch

Rep impersonation resolves by `vendor_id` when the request body has `{ targetType: 'vendor' }`; visible factory-row impersonation resolves `Factory.id` when `targetType` is `factory` and can produce a vendor-scoped token with `email: null`. |
| `Factory` | `factory_id`, `factory_nickname`, `factory_country` | `models/db/Factory.js` | `factory_country` added May 2026 via safe migration; backfilled from most common `vendor_country` |
| `RFQVendor` | `RFQVendor_id`, `RFQitem_id_fk`, `vendor_id_fk`, `RFQVendor_amount`, `quote_date`, `req_status`, `requote_requested` | `models/db/RFQVendor.js` | One factory's quote on one RFQ item. `RFQVendor_id` is the `quote_id` the frontend sends. **`vendor_id_fk` stores `Factory.id`, not `Vendor.vendor_id`** (see §11). `requote_requested` (BOOLEAN, added 2026-06 via safe migration) flags a stale price for refresh; mapped in `helpers/utility.js` `remapRFQItem`. |
| `RFQItem` | `rfqItem_id`, `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation`, `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, `Price Code` | `models/db/RFQItem.js` | `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` stores the raw 3-term duty breakdown (e.g. `"5.3+25+10"`). `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` (TEXT/JSON) maps each `*_price_sales` colId → `{duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at}`, snapshotting the cost inputs when a "Price for Sales" cell was populated; mapped both directions in `helpers/utility.js` (`remapRFQItem`/`mapRFQItem`), mirroring `rfqItem_requested_price_cells`. |
| `app_settings` | `key`, `value` (VARCHAR, stores JSON) | `models/db/AppSetting.js` | Managed by `services/app-setting.service.js`; see §6 for key inventory |
| `user_notification` | `id`, `type`, `event`, `title`, `message`, `unread`, `user_id_fk` | `models/db/UserNotification.js` | Managed by `services/notification.service.js` |

**`app_settings` key inventory:**

| Key | Value shape | Written by |

| `ai-model` | OpenRouter model ID string | Admin → AI Settings |

payload.output:
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.3 seconds
Output:

---

## Cost tracking

Per-turn cost and session total are computed from the token usage returned by the API.

**Per-token pricing** is loaded once per dialog open from the model list endpoint (`GET /api/openrouter/models?api=...`). The `OpenRouterModel.pricing` shape is:

```ts
{ prompt: string; completion: string; prompt_cache?: string }
```

All three are dollar-per-token strings in OpenRouter's convention (`"0.00000014"` = $0.14/1M tokens). `prompt_cache` is the cache-hit price — approximately 10% of the `prompt` miss price for DeepSeek.

**`computeTurnCost(usage)`** in `duty_rate_dialog.component.ts`:
- When cache telemetry is available (`prompt_cache_hit_tokens` + `prompt_cache_miss_tokens`): split pricing — cache hits charged at `prompt_cache` rate, misses at `prompt` rate.
- Otherwise: total `prompt_tokens` × `prompt` rate.
- Adds `completion_tokens` × `completion` rate.

**`sessionCostUsd`** accumulates across all turns for the dialog lifetime. Displayed in the AI hint bar at the bottom: `· Session cost: $X.XXXX`.

Per-turn cost appears in each turn's usage line: `3,420 cached + 412 new · cache hit 89% · $0.0042`. Hidden when pricing is unavailable (model not in the list or no pricing field returned).

---

## Model API routing (OpenRouter vs. DeepSeek direct)

The AI Settings admin page has two dropdowns: **AI API** (`OpenRouter` or `DeepSeek Direct`) and **AI Model** (populated from the selected API's model list).

**OpenRouter** routes through OpenRouter's proxy to any model in the catalog.

**DeepSeek Direct** routes to DeepSeek's API at `api.deepseek.com` using `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`, via `helpers/deepseek.js` on the backend. Two models are hardcoded:

| Model ID | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` | Thinking mode, best HTS accuracy. Supports `reasoning_effort: high \| max`. |
| `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` | Fast, no reasoning. For lower-cost classification or `suggestCrossTerms()` calls. |

The `api` field is sent with every `/api/openrouter/chat` and `/api/openrouter/models` request. When `api = 'deepseek-direct'`, the backend routes to `helpers/deepseek.js`; otherwise `helpers/openrouter.js`. Auto-routing: if `api` is omitted and the model ID starts with `deepseek/` and `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` is set, direct routing is used automatically.

**Why direct instead of via OpenRouter for DeepSeek:** Lower latency, no OpenRouter markup, direct access to the `thinking` mode `reasoning_effort` parameter.

---

## Three-part duty-rate breakdown

The duty-rate dialog presents three editable fields — MFN base, Section 301 (China), Trump 2025 reciprocal — instead of a single combined percent. Each field is auto-populated from its own authoritative lookup; the user can override any of them before saving.

**Why three fields instead of one total:**

- Future auditability. Six months later, "40.3%" tells you nothing. "5.3 + 25 + 10" tells you the MFN component, the Section 301 portion, and the reciprocal tier.
- Independent updates. When the Trump 2025 rate for Vietnam changes, the admin updates the table once; new classifications pick up the new rate without changing MFN or Section 301.
- Verification. A user can spot a wrong component (e.g. "Section 301 shouldn't apply — this isn't from China") in the dialog before saving.

---

## Model selection guidance

Configured in **Editor → AI Settings**. Recommendations by use case:

**OpenRouter models (via AI API = OpenRouter):**

| Model (OpenRouter ID) | Best for | Prompt caching |
|----------------------|----------|---------------|
| `deepseek/deepseek-chat` | Cost-sensitive single-model | Yes (DeepSeek) |
| `deepseek/deepseek-r1` | Reasoning model (thinks step-by-step) | Yes |
| `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5` | Fast, cheap interviewer | Yes (Anthropic) |
| `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5` | Best accuracy/cost single-model | Yes |
| `meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct` | No caching, but free tier | No |
| `google/gemini-flash-1.5` | Large context, cheap | Yes (Google) |

**DeepSeek Direct models (via AI API = DeepSeek Direct, requires `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`):**

| Model ID | Best for | Reasoning |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` | Best HTS accuracy; thinking mode | `reasoning_effort: high \| max` |
| `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` | Faster, cheaper; also used for `suggestCrossTerms()` | None |

> The admin model picker loads the live list from `GET /api/openrouter/models?api=openrouter|deepseek-direct`
> so you always see what is actually available for the selected API.

---

## Files involved

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/app/pages/rfq/duty_rate_dialog/duty_rate_dialog.component.*` | Chat UI, CROSS search panel, three-field breakdown form, equation builder |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/desc-cleaner.ts` | Regex description cleanup |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/fob-country-autofill.ts` | Pure rule for filling FOB Country from a factory pick (never clobbers existing) |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` | Opens the dialog; stores evaluated total in `[colField].title`, equation in `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` + `[colField]_equation` |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/detail-cell-renderer/detail-cell-renderer.component.ts` | Sub-grid; loads factory→country map once; invokes `computeFobAutofill` on Name change |
| `src/app/helpers/services/hts-classifier.service.ts` | AI chat, `buildInitialMessages()`, `parseResponse()`, `suggestCrossTerms()` |
| `src/app/helpers/services/cross.service.ts` | CROSS API client |
| `src/app/helpers/services/ai-settings.service.ts` | Model config + `getModelInfo()` (for pricing) + Section 301 + HTS-rates + Trump 2025 + Factories APIs |
| `src/app/helpers/math-expr-cell-editor/math-expr-cell-editor.component.ts` | Excel-style cell editor; stores raw equation in `data[colId + '_equation']` |
| `src/app/pages/editor/ai-settings/ai-settings.component.*` | Admin UI: model picker |
| `src/app/pages/editor/section301-data/section301-data.component.*` | Admin UI: Section 301 CSV upload |
| `src/app/pages/editor/section301-overrides/section301-overrides.component.*` | Admin UI: per-code Section 301 corrections |
| `src/app/pages/editor/trump-2025-tariffs/trump-2025-tariffs.component.*` | Admin UI: per-country Trump 2025 rate table editor |
| `src/app/pages/editor/factory-country/factory-country.component.*` | Admin UI: per-factory country editor with search and dirty-state highlight |
| `helpers/openrouter.js` (backend) | OpenRouter chat + models proxy (`listUserModels`, `chatCompletion`) |
| `helpers/deepseek.js` (backend) | DeepSeek direct API client; maps `reasoning_effort` to DeepSeek `thinking` param |
| `helpers/cross.js` (backend) | CBP CROSS proxy |
| `helpers/section301-csv.js` (backend) | Preferred CSV parser for USTR all-lists export |
| `helpers/section301.js` (backend) | Legacy PDF text extraction + regex HTS parsing; lazy-requires `pdf-parse` |
| `helpers/hts-rates.js` (backend) | MFN base-rate lookup against bundled USITC JSON; rate parsing |
| `helpers/trump2025.js` (backend) | Trump 2025 country lookup with alias matching; list validation |
| `helpers/factories.js` (backend) | Factory list, country map, single-factory country update |
| `data/hts-rates.json` (backend) | Bundled USITC HTS schedule snapshot (~1 MB, ~13.6k codes) |
| `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` (backend) | One-shot regenerator for `data/hts-rates.json` from USITC |
| `routes/openrouter.router.js` (backend) | `/api/openrouter/models`, `/api/openrouter/chat` |
| `routes/admin.router.js` (backend) | All admin endpoints (`section301`, `hts-rates`, `trump2025`, `factories`, `app-settings`) |
| `controllers/admin.controller.js` (backend) | All admin controller functions |
| `services/app-setting.service.js` (backend) | `app_settings` DB read/write — stores `ai-model`, `section301_lookup`, `section301_overrides`, `trump_2025_tariffs` |

---

## Prompt architecture — classification methodology

The system prompt encodes **reasoning principles**, never product-to-code mappings.
Adding hardcoded mappings ("anti-fatigue mat → 3924") does not generalize; a reasoning
principle does. When the model makes a wrong classification:

1. Correct it via the chat input
2. Ask: "what reasoning principle, stated generally, would have gotten you to the right
   answer from the start?"
3. Extract the general principle (not the specific product) and add it to the
   classification methodology section of the system prompt in `hts-classifier.service.ts`

**Current 5-step methodology in the prompt:**
1. Search CROSS rulings FIRST — a binding CBP ruling is the primary authority. Check
   every provided ruling; if one covers the same type of article, use its HTS code.
   Only proceed to Step 2 when no ruling clearly settles the classification.
2. Read the product's own name as a classification signal — use-context modifiers
   ("kitchen", "bath", "workshop") signal the heading before physical form is considered
3. Read the heading text literally: does it describe what the product IS, not where it
   is placed or what it resembles?
4. List all plausible headings before choosing; apply GRI 3a/3b
5. Ask about installed vs. portable when that distinction drives the heading choice

**Confidence rule:** the model is forbidden from returning a classification with
confidence "medium" or "low". A client-side guard in `callAi()` intercepts any
non-high-confidence response that slips through and automatically continues the
conversation (capped at 4 retries).

**Response types** (all returned as raw JSON, no surrounding text):
- `{"type":"question","text":"one question"}` — when more information is needed
- `{"type":"classification","hts_code":"...","confidence":"high","reasoning":"...","suggested_duty_pct":null}`
- `{"type":"explanation","text":"..."}` — for corrections and follow-up questions

---

## Compound rate handling

Some USITC rates are compound: `"3.4% + $0.25/pc"`, `"10% + 12¢/pc"`, `"Free + $0.35/piece"`.
`parseCompoundRate()` in `src/app/helpers/hts-compound-rate.ts` extracts:
- `percent` — ad-valorem % (auto-fills MFN field)
- `dollarPerPiece` — per-piece specific duty (auto-fills dollar-duty field)
- `hasNonPieceSpecific` — true for $/kg, $/dozen etc. (warns user to enter manually)

---

## Open work (implementation order)

- [x] CROSS search panel in duty rate dialog (basic, no AI)
- [x] OpenRouter backend proxy (chat + models)
- [x] App settings DB table + admin model picker page
- [x] Description cleaner (`desc-cleaner.ts` + tests)
- [x] AI chat panel in duty rate dialog (single-model path)
- [x] Section 301 CSV upload, overrides, legacy PDF parsing, and lookup
- [x] MFN base rate from bundled USITC HTS schedule (`hts-rates.js` + `data/hts-rates.json`)
- [x] Three-field duty-rate breakdown (MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025); equation persisted alongside total
- [x] Trump 2025 admin page + alias-aware lookup
- [x] Factory country column + admin page + sub-grid FOB auto-fill
- [x] Compound rate parsing and auto-fill (`hts-compound-rate.ts`)
- [x] High-confidence-only enforcement + client-side retry guard
- [x] Chat follow-up and correction handling (`explanation` response type)
- [x] 5-step classification methodology + name-as-signal principle in system prompt
- [x] Automated known-context injection (FOB country, Trump rate)
- [x] CBP CROSS as primary classification authority (Step 1 in system prompt; before heading analysis)
- [x] Smart-quote dimension normalization in `desc-cleaner.ts` (Step 0; fixes `x12mm` artifact)
- [x] AI-driven CROSS retry — `suggestCrossTerms()` suggests shorter/alternate terms when zero results
- [x] Loading spinner visible during full `initAiSession()` setup (not just `callAi()`)
- [x] Per-turn cost display and session total (`computeTurnCost()`, `sessionCostUsd`)
- [x] DeepSeek direct API routing (`helpers/deepseek.js`, `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro/flash`)
- [ ] RAG: keyword search over hts-rates.json for relevant chapter notes (step 1 — no new infra)
- [ ] RAG: vector embeddings over full HTS schedule (step 2 — requires pgvector or similar)
- [ ] Optional: Multi-model routing for Stage 1/2/3 split (only if accuracy insufficient in production)

+There is no separate migration job. A schema change ships by adding an `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` or `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` statement to `models/db.js`; the new column/table is only realized in an environment once the new revision boots.
+
+### AI cache telemetry startup table
+
+What changed:
+`ai_cache_events` is created at startup and stores one row per AI provider response for aggregate cache diagnostics. It is intentionally append-only and indexed by `created_at` and `(feature, created_at)`.
+
+Why:
+Admin AI Settings needs historical cache utilization across classifications, not only per-dialog usage lines.
+
+Future sessions should:
+When modifying this table, update `models/db/AiCacheEvent.js`, the idempotent startup SQL in `models/db.js`, and `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` together. Do not rely on `sequelize.sync()` to alter existing deployed tables.

Update only the Markdown files that need durable knowledge from this specific session.

Goal

Record what this session learned or changed so future developers and future AI sessions do not have to rediscover it.

Focus on:

Information from this session that will still matter later.

Idiosyncrasies that may look wrong but are intentional.

Mistakes, dead ends, or assumptions from this session that future sessions should avoid.

Architecture, data flow, deployment, configuration, or workflow changes.

New operational steps, rollback notes, feature flags, identifiers, limits, or dependencies.

Any temporary/incomplete work that needs a handoff.


Rules

Derive every doc update from code, config, migrations, workflows, commands run, or verified session findings.

Do not document guesses as facts. Mark unknowns clearly and say how to verify them.

Do not add secrets, tokens, passwords, private keys, or credential values.

Do not duplicate large explanations across multiple docs. Put the short routing/context note in AGENTS.md only if future AI sessions need to see it early; put topic detail in the relevant doc under docs/.

If the change does not require documentation updates, say so explicitly in the final report.


What To Update

AGENTS.md: only for high-signal guidance future AI sessions must see quickly, including new quirks, critical warnings, task routing, identifiers, or "do not repeat this mistake" notes.

HANDOFF.md: create or update only if work is unfinished, blocked, partially deployed, or requires continuation context. Delete it only when the unfinished work it describes is truly complete.

Topic docs under docs/: update the specific doc for the affected area, such as architecture, deployment, configuration, schema, worker logic, PopSG, Helper, Seafile, auth, bulk jobs, or known quirks.

README.md: update only if the quick-start or top-level orientation changed.

CLAUDE.md: update only for Claude Code-specific workflow rules. General AI/developer guidance belongs in AGENTS.md.


Required Session Note Shape

When adding session-derived knowledge, prefer concise entries like:

### [Short decision or warning name]

What changed:
[One or two sentences.]

Why:
[Constraint, incident, bug, user decision, or operational reason.]

Future sessions should:
[Concrete instruction: what to do, what to avoid, where to verify.]

For unfinished work, include:

## [Workstream name]

Status:
[done / partial / blocked / not started]

Done:
[Verified completed items.]

Next action:
[Exact next step.]

Risks / watchouts:
[Anything future sessions must not miss.]

Verification Before Final Response

Docs changed are limited to files that actually needed updates.

# AI Session Documentation Update Prompt

Use this at the end of an AI coding session when the session changed code, deployment behavior, data flow, configuration, operational assumptions, or important project knowledge.

Your job is not to rebuild the entire documentation system. 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Update only the Markdown files that need durable knowledge from this specific session.

## Goal

Record what this session learned or changed so future developers and future AI sessions do not have to rediscover it.

Focus on:
- Information from this session that will still matter later.
- Idiosyncrasies that may look wrong but are intentional.
- Mistakes, dead ends, or assumptions from this session that future sessions should avoid.
- Architecture, data flow, deployment, configuration, or workflow changes.
- New operational steps, rollback notes, feature flags, identifiers, limits, or dependencies.
- Any temporary/incomplete work that needs a handoff.

## Rules

- Derive every doc update from code, config, migrations, workflows, commands run, or verified session findings.
- Do not document guesses as facts. Mark unknowns clearly and say how to verify them.
- Do not add secrets, tokens, passwords, private keys, or credential values.
- Do not duplicate large explanations across multiple docs. Put the short routing/context note in `AGENTS.md` only if future AI sessions need to see it early; put topic detail in the relevant doc under `docs/`.
- If the change does not require documentation updates, say so explicitly in the final report.

## What To Update

- `AGENTS.md`: only for high-signal guidance future AI sessions must see quickly, including new quirks, critical warnings, task routing, identifiers, or "do not repeat this mistake" notes.
- `HANDOFF.md`: create or update only if work is unfinished, blocked, partially deployed, or requires continuation context. Delete it only when the unfinished work it describes is truly complete.
- Topic docs under `docs/`: update the specific doc for the affected area, such as architecture, deployment, configuration, schema, worker logic, PopSG, Helper, Seafile, auth, bulk jobs, or known quirks.
- `README.md`: update only if the quick-start or top-level orientation changed.
- `CLAUDE.md`: update only for Claude Code-specific workflow rules. General AI/developer guidance belongs in `AGENTS.md`.

## Required Session Note Shape

When adding session-derived knowledge, prefer concise entries like:

```md
### [Short decision or warning name]

What changed:
[One or two sentences.]

Why:
[Constraint, incident, bug, user decision, or operational reason.]

Future sessions should:
[Concrete instruction: what to do, what to avoid, where to verify.]
```

For unfinished work, include:

```md
## [Workstream name]

Status:
[done / partial / blocked / not started]

Done:
[Verified completed items.]

Next action:
[Exact next step.]

Risks / watchouts:
[Anything future sessions must not miss.]
```

## Verification Before Final Response

- Docs changed are limited to files that actually needed updates.
- New claims match the repository or verified session findings.
- No secrets were added.
- `HANDOFF.md` exists only if continuation context is needed.
- Any new quirk or warning explains why it exists and what would break if ignored.

## Final Report

Reply with:

```md
## Documentation Updates
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `path/to/file.md` | Short summary |

## Handoff
- `HANDOFF.md`: present/absent
- Reason: ...

## Verification
- Docs verified against session findings: yes/no
- Secrets added: yes/no
- Unknowns remaining: ...
```
